data.title: Middle East's Luxury Watch Market Faces Challenges Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: The Middle East, once a thriving market for luxury timepieces, is experiencing significant challenges due to the ongoing conflict in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting how the region's demand for high-end watches has been impacted by the geopolitical tensions. The luxury watch industry, which had seen growth in the Middle East, is now facing uncertainty as the conflict disrupts economic stability and consumer confidence. Retailers and manufacturers are closely monitoring the situation, as the prolonged instability could further affect sales and market dynamics. The luxury sector, known for its resilience, is adapting to these challenges by exploring alternative markets and strategies to sustain growth.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 76,000 USDT with a 2.00% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 17, 2026, 09:29 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 76,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 76,349.992188 USDT, with a narrowed 2.00% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: A whale purchased 4.5 million LDOs via OTC, worth $1.86 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, a whale purchased 4.5 million LDO tokens worth $1.86 million via OTC and received the same amount of LDO and 10,000 AAVE tokens worth $1.15 million from Wintermute and FalconX in the past day or so.

data.title: Bitcoin Whale Opens High-Leverage Position with No Stop-Loss
data.description:
A significant Bitcoin transaction was observed on April 17, involving a whale address starting with 0x133. According to BlockBeats, the address opened a long position on Bitcoin using 40x leverage, with a position size of $6.95 million at an average price of $75,320. 

After achieving an approximate 20% unrealized profit, the whale placed a limit order to close the entire position at $77,000. Notably, the address did not set any stop-loss orders, suggesting the liquidation price of $74,287 might serve as an implicit stop-loss level.

data.title: SoSoValue to Unlock 15.82 Million Tokens on April 24
data.description: SoSoValue (SOSO) is set to unlock approximately 15.82 million tokens on April 24 at 10:00 AM UTC+8. According to ChainCatcher, this token release is part of the scheduled unlock events tracked by the Web3 asset data platform, RootData.

data.title: Mentis Product Director to Attend Crypto 2026 Forum in Hong Kong
data.description:
Mentis Product Director Jerry is set to participate in the 'Crypto 2026: From Cryptocurrency to Smart Economy' forum in Hong Kong on April 19. According to ChainCatcher, this event is part of the Hong Kong Web3 Carnival and is co-hosted by ChainCatcher and RootData.

Mentis, an on-chain AI assistant, aims to integrate artificial intelligence with blockchain data to enhance market understanding and signal detection for users. The forum will focus on the paradigm shift resulting from the deep integration of AI technology and crypto networks, with discussions on AI Agent economy, smart payments, RWA, and crypto infrastructure. This event aims to provide insights into the current industry landscape and offer forward-thinking perspectives on the future of the smart economy.

data.title: South African Reserve Bank Governor Comments on Oil Price Impact Due to Iran Conflict
data.description: South African Reserve Bank Governor Lesetja Kganyago has expressed concerns about the impact of the Iran conflict on global oil prices, highlighting the potential inflationary pressures. Bloomberg posted on X that Kganyago emphasized the importance of monitoring geopolitical developments, as they can significantly influence economic stability. The ongoing situation in Iran has led to fluctuations in oil prices, which in turn affect inflation rates worldwide. Kganyago's remarks underscore the need for vigilance in economic policy to mitigate the effects of such external shocks on the economy.

data.title: SIREN Surges 151% Before Pullback, Traders Eye Fibonacci Retracement
data.description: SIREN experienced a significant surge of 151% on Thursday, reaching a peak of $2.25 before retreating to approximately $1.66. According to NS3.AI, traders are closely monitoring the $1.37 Fibonacci retracement level as a potential first test of demand following the rally.

data.title: Following the news of Binance's delisting announcement, DEGO, DENT, and TRU all briefly fell by more than 20%.
data.description: Market data shows that tokens delisted from Binance all experienced short-term drops exceeding 20%. Specifically: DEGO briefly fell below 0.25 USDT, currently trading at 0.252 USDT, a 10% drop in the last 24 hours; DENT fell below 0.00015 USDT, a 16.97% drop in the last 24 hours; and TRU briefly fell below 0.006 USDT, a 13.24% drop in the last 24 hours. Previous reports indicated that Binance would delist the DEGO, DENT, and TRU trading pairs.

data.title: South Korean Lawmaker Urges Focus on Stablecoin Legislation
data.description:
A South Korean lawmaker has expressed concerns over equity restrictions on cryptocurrency exchanges, urging a focus on stablecoin legislation. According to Odaily, Kim Sang-hoon, head of the Digital Assets Special Committee of the People Power Party, highlighted these issues during an academic conference. He emphasized the need to prioritize the second phase of digital asset basic law related to stablecoins to avoid missing a crucial legislative window due to regulatory disputes over equity.

Kim pointed out the emergence of the KRWQ, a stablecoin pegged to the South Korean won, as a significant challenge to monetary sovereignty. He stressed that legislation should provide market predictability, clear guidance for operators, and effective security mechanisms for users.

data.title: Kim Sang-hoon Urges Swift Enactment of Digital Asset Basic Act Phase Two
data.description: Kim Sang-hoon has advocated for the rapid implementation of the second phase of the Digital Asset Basic Act, emphasizing the importance of stablecoins. According to NS3.AI, Kim suggested that the legislation should not include the proposed limitations on crypto exchange ownership.

data.title: Stablecoin Demand Rises Amid Crypto Market Weakness
data.description: Stablecoin demand is increasing despite the overall weakness in the cryptocurrency market. According to NS3.AI, Darkfost reported that average weekly inflows into Nexo have grown from approximately $8 million to $15 million since February. This trend indicates that investors are utilizing stablecoins to manage idle funds and explore interest-bearing opportunities.

data.title: AI TRENDS | ASML and TSMC Report Strong Earnings, Highlighting Robust AI Demand
data.description: ASML's first-quarter earnings met market expectations, and the company has raised its revenue guidance for 2026. According to Jin10, UBS noted on April 16 that ASML typically revises its guidance around mid-year, making this upward adjustment significant as it indicates increased visibility of strong demand under long-term supply agreements. Additionally, TSMC reported a 58% increase in first-quarter profits, surpassing expectations and setting a new record. TSMC also forecasts that its second-quarter sales will reach new highs and stated that this year's capital expenditures will be at the high end of its previous guidance range. UBS believes that the performances of ASML and TSMC confirm the ongoing strong demand for AI computing as AI applications continue to proliferate. UBS also mentioned that major U.S. tech companies will release their earnings in the coming weeks, with investors closely watching their monetization efforts and outcomes. The bank remains optimistic about AI as a long-term trend and advises investors to diversify across the AI value chain and different regions.

data.title: SIREN plummeted by 80% in a short period, falling more than 46% within the day.
data.description: Market data shows that SIREN experienced a sharp drop in a short period, with a decline of up to 80% at one point. It is currently trading at $0.46, with a daily drop of over 46%.

data.title: The Hong Kong Stock Exchange has released a consultation paper on shortening the settlement cycle of the Hong Kong stock spot market.
data.description: The Hong Kong Stock Exchange today published a consultation paper (the "Consultation Paper") on shortening the settlement cycle of the Hong Kong stock spot market, proposing a suggested operating model to reduce the settlement cycle from the current T+2 to T+1, and soliciting market feedback on the proposal. (Jinshi)

data.title: French Finance Minister Advocates for Euro-Based Stablecoins
data.description: French Finance Minister Roland Lescure has emphasized the need for more euro-based stablecoins in Europe. According to Foresight News, Lescure encouraged regional banks to explore tokenized deposits, highlighting the disparity between the size of euro stablecoins and their dollar counterparts. He expressed dissatisfaction with the current situation, noting that euro stablecoins are significantly smaller compared to those based on the U.S. dollar.

data.title: AI Outperforms Humans in Yield Optimization
data.description: Artificial intelligence has demonstrated superior performance compared to humans in the field of yield optimization. According to PANews, AI systems have achieved an annualized return of 9.75% through methods such as ARMA. This advancement highlights the growing capabilities of AI in financial applications, where it continues to surpass traditional human strategies.

data.title: South Korean lawmakers are calling for prioritizing stablecoin legislation to avoid delays caused by equity restriction disputes.
data.description: Kim Sang-hoon, a lawmaker and chairman of the Digital Assets Special Committee of the People Power Party of South Korea, expressed concern about equity restrictions on cryptocurrency exchanges at an academic conference. He called for prioritizing the enactment of a basic law on digital assets related to stablecoins (the second phase of legislation) to avoid missing the golden opportunity for legislation due to equity regulation disputes. He pointed out that the issuance and circulation of the Korean won stablecoin KRWQ overseas poses a serious challenge to monetary sovereignty. Kim emphasized that the law should grant market predictability, provide clear guidance for operators, and offer effective security mechanisms for users. (Edaily)
